title: Entertainment
outcomes: Discuss how you spend your free time
Discuss different films and TV series
grammar_in_lexis: Adjectives and adverbs of degree (extreme adjectives with really/absolutely)

subtopics.title: Talking about films, music and books

examples_book: a great song with a catchy chorus
extra_contexts: a catchy TikTok jingle stuck in my head all day
the brand needs a catchier tagline
her campaign slogan is annoyingly catchy
my Monday meetings are hilariously chaotic
his impression of the boss is absolutely hilarious
she posted a hilarious take on the news

collocates: the film was a flop commercially
extra_contexts: her indie album turned surprisingly commercial
streaming has made everything more commercial
a commercially safe sequel

collocates: viewers may find some scenes disturbing
extra_contexts: I found the news alert disturbing

weirdly, no one replied to the group chat
an astonishing turnaround in the second half

his portfolio grew at an astonishing rate

an absolutely dreadful first draft
she's dreading the interview

her screen time is finally under control
everything is on demand now, nothing is scheduled

he's been glued to his laptop all afternoon
kids glued to their tablets on the flight
the keynote was surprisingly inspiring
an inspiring LinkedIn post that actually meant something

collocates: the remake was a total flop
extra_contexts: yet another Disney live-action remake
the reboot is basically a remake with better VFX

extension_chunks.chunk: stuff like that / that kind of stuff
extra_contexts: we watched documentaries and stuff like that
there's a bunch of stuff I need to sort out

extension_chunks.chunk: burst into tears / be in tears
extra_contexts: I nearly burst into tears at the ceremony
she was close to tears reading the email
the sequel bombed at the box office

his life revolves around making art

the whole meeting revolved around Q4 targets

extra_contexts: life took a weird twist that year

extra_contexts: her second album feels like a sequel to the first

the stage adaptation was tighter than the book

collocates: one of my all-time favourite classics
extra_contexts: a modern classic
it's a classic mistake we all make on our first job
the song became an unexpected smash hit on TikTok
her podcast is a smash hit with under-30s

core_chunks.chunk: driven by sth / a driven person
extra_contexts: she's driven by curiosity, not money
a decision driven by market pressure

collocates: heavily reliant on box office success
extra_contexts: we're too reliant on Slack
a business model reliant on ad revenue
how are you going to tackle that inbox?
the government failed to tackle inflation

her essay touches on identity without preaching

core_chunks.chunk: a sharp satire on sth / sharp criticism
extra_contexts: a sharp satire on hustle culture
the review was sharply worded

extra_contexts: mounting tension in the group chat

extension_chunks.chunk: an amazing cast / be cast as
a strong desire to do sth / desire for
extension_chunks.pattern: desire TO + verb / desire FOR + noun

extension_chunks.chunk: shoot a TV series / the film was shot in…
just the tip of the iceberg

the report is too abstract, we need specifics

the CEO's answer was carefully ambiguous

beneath the surface, the team was exhausted

collocates: the therapist was depicted as calm

extra_contexts: the media depicted her as reckless
the ad depicts working parents as heroes
a dramatic drop in engagement

core_chunks.chunk: in the foreground ↔ in the background
collocates: in the foreground of the picture
a small cat in the foreground
extra_contexts: in the foreground of the report: user churn
background music vs foreground focus

collocates: in the heat of the moment
extra_contexts: a heated Slack thread about the decision

core_chunks.chunk: get the impression that / a vivid impression
extra_contexts: I got the distinct impression she was joking

her feedback was open to interpretation

the media landscape has changed
the political landscape shifted overnight
a subtle hint in her message

extra_contexts: the underlying issue is trust, not process

prep_after_adj.description: Adjective + fixed preposition. Common B2 error zone. Learn as a whole chunk, not as 'rule'.

examples: the ending is open to interpretation
the plot revolves around a heist
a sequel to the first film
a sharp satire on hustle culture

items.chunk: in the foreground / background OF sth
examples: in the background of the photo

extreme_adjectives.description: Ungradable adjectives — use really/absolutely, NOT very. TB p.23.

habits_grammar.description: Grammar exponents for talking about present and past habits. Taught as chunks, not rules.

items.chunk: tend to / tend not to + verb
examples: I tend to watch films on demand
I tend not to go out during the week
As a rule, I don't drink coffee after 5
I go running now and then

items.chunk: used to + verb (past habit no longer true)
examples: I used to be glued to Instagram
We'd binge a whole series in a weekend
My headphones are glued to my ears all the time

al_suffix.description: Noun → Adjective with -al suffix. From VB p.8 meta-block.
